200.a: A Norse farmer who rises to become King of Denmark and the most famous Viking raider

200.q: Who is Lagertha?
200.a: Ragnar's first wife — a shieldmaiden and eventually an earl and queen

200.q: Who is Floki?
200.a: Ragnar's eccentric best friend — a legendary boat builder devoted to the Norse gods

200.q: What is the first place Ragnar raids in the West?
200.a: Lindisfarne — a monastery in England — in 793 AD

200.q: Who is Rollo?
200.a: Ragnar's brother — a powerful warrior who eventually sides against him

200.q: Who is Bjorn Ironside?
200.a: Ragnar's son — who becomes a legendary Viking leader in his own right

200.q: What is the Thing?
200.a: The Norse assembly where free men gather to make decisions and settle disputes

200.q: What are berserkers?
200.a: Viking warriors who enter a trance-like battle fury — associated with Odin

200.q: Who is Athelstan?
200.a: An Anglo-Saxon monk taken captive by Ragnar — they form a complex spiritual friendship

200.q: What is Valhalla?
200.a: The Norse afterlife for warriors who die in battle — ruled by Odin

Who is King Ecbert?
400.a: The ambitious King of Wessex — one of the most politically sophisticated characters in the show

400.q: What is Paris and why is it significant?
400.a: The Vikings besiege Paris in Seasons 3-4 — a historically based major event

400.q: Who is Aslaug?
400.a: Ragnar's second wife — a völva (seer) and princess of legendary descent

400.q: Who is Ivar the Boneless?
400.a: Ragnar's most vicious son — he is carried due to a physical condition but becomes the most ruthless leader

400.q: What is the significance of Rollo settling in France?
400.a: He becomes the first Duke of Normandy — ancestor of William the Conqueror

400.q: Who is King Aelle?
400.a: The King of Northumbria who captures and kills Ragnar via the blood eagle

400.q: What is the blood eagle?
400.a: A brutal Norse execution ritual — the ribs are cut from the spine and lungs pulled out to form wings

400.q: Who is Halfdan the Black?
400.a: Harald Finehair's wild brother — a fearless raider who explores the world with Bjorn

400.q: Who kills Ragnar Lothbrok?
400.a: King Aelle — he throws Ragnar into a pit of snakes

400.q: What is the aftermath of Ragnar's death?
400.a: His sons launch the Great Heathen Army to avenge him — invading England

600.a: A massive Viking invasion of England in 865 AD — led by Ragnar's sons — historically documented

600.q: What is the significance of Floki sailing to Iceland?
600.a: He discovers a land he believes is the home of the gods — the Vikings settle Iceland in the later seasons

600.q: What is the connection between Vikings and Vikings: Valhalla?
600.a: Valhalla is a Netflix sequel set 100 years later — featuring Leif Eriksson and Harald Sigurdsson

600.q: Who is Harald Finehair?
600.a: King of Norway who seeks to unite all Norse kingdoms — an ally and rival to Bjorn

600.q: What is the historical accuracy level of the show?
600.a: It uses real historical figures and events but takes significant liberties — it inspired popular interest in Norse history

600.q: Who is Prince Oleg of Novgorod?
600.a: A Byzantine-influenced Russian ruler — the primary villain of Seasons 5-6, enemies with Ivar then Bjorn

600.q: What is Hvitserk's arc?
600.a: Ragnar's most troubled son — he converts to Christianity and struggles with addiction and guilt

600.q: What is the significance of Ubbe?
600.a: He fulfils Ragnar's dream by sailing to North America — settling Vinland

600.q: What is the legacy of Ragnar Lothbrok in real history?
600.a: A semi-legendary Norse hero — his sons' Great Heathen Army is historically documented; his existence is debated

600.q: Who is Judith?
600.a: Athelstan's lover and Aethelwulf's wife — she plays a major political role in the later seasons
